channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge has no choice but to grant the Justice Department’s unusual and divisive request to dismiss New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, a court-appointed lawyer said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Violent and sudden. What a firing squad execution looked like through my eyes
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — I've now watched through glass and bars as 11 men were put to death at a South Carolina prison. None of the previous 10 prepared me for watching the firing squad death of Brad Sigmon on Friday night. I might now be unique among U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A timeline of how actor Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa died at their New Mexico home
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Actor Gene Hackman died of heart disease a full week after his wife died from hantavirus in their New Mexico home, likely unaware that she was dead because he was in the advanced stages of Alzheimer’s disease, authorities revealed Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Convicted double murderer executed by firing squad in South Carolina
item.description: A South Carolina man convicted of brutally murdering a couple in 2001 was executed by firing squad Friday, becoming the first U.S. prisoner to die by the method in 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defense Department begins staffing cuts as 31,000 employees offer to resign: report
item.description: A few thousand Department of Defense employees are expected to be terminated as part of the Trump administration's effort to downsize the agency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louisiana student died after being punched in hazing ritual, police say
item.description: Baton Rouge police confirmed Southern University student Caleb Wilson died after being punched in the chest multiple times during a hazing ritual. One arrest has been made, and more suspects are expected.
item.dc:creator: Keymonte Avery, KRON4

item.title: Mexican cartel leader’s son is sentenced to life in prison for role in major drug trafficking plot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Mexican drug cartel boss was sentenced Friday to life in prison for helping his father run one of the country’s largest and most violent narcotics trafficking organizations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tony! Toni! Tone! guitarist D'Wayne Wiggins dies
item.description: D'Wayne Wiggins, guitarist and founding member of Tony! Toni! Tone!, has died, according to a social media post from his family. The guitarist, who was a native of Oakland, had been battling bladder cancer.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Moon mission goes sideways: Private lunar lander declared dead after botched landing
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon’s south pole and its mission is over, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: By MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to resume detaining migrant families in Texas facilities
item.description: President Donald Trump will resume detaining migrant families as part of his administration's mass deportation effort
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Compounders must stop making copies of Eli Lilly's weight loss drugs: Judge
item.description: Compounders had been allowed to produce and sell hundreds of thousands of their own versions of popular anti-obesity drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Masked home invader 'shot' after 'pistol-whipping' OnlyFans star, demanding cryptocurrency
item.description: OnlyFans model Kaitlyn “Amouranth" Siragusa provided real-time updates to her followers as armed intruders stormed her Texas home and demanded access to her cryptocurrency accounts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: Trump, who once called crypto a "scam," has drastically changed his tune on the subject and promised on the campaign trail to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at Indiana post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs fans' cause of death revealed after being found frozen in friend's yard
item.description: The three Kansas City Chiefs fans found frozen in their friend's backyard died of fentanyl and cocaine toxicity, as new details about their bizarre deaths unravel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, Fox 5

item.title: Flight attendant pleads guilty to secretly recording a 14-year-old girl in plane bathroom
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A former American Airlines flight attendant has pleaded guilty to secretly recording video of a 14-year-old girl using an airplane bathroom and having recordings of four other girls using the lavatories.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: List of Social Security Administration offices DOGE is closing
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency has started to make large budget cuts, resulting in the closure of multiple SSA offices.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: House education chair backs Trump move to abolish federal agency and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Medical plane’s voice recorder likely wasn’t working for years before Philadelphia crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The cockpit voice recorder was not working on a medical transport plane that killed seven people when it plummeted into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January and likely had not been functioning for several years, the National Transportation Safety Board said in a preliminary rep
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Authorities in Alaska said they would attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers, who were believed to have died after being buried in snow.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Spin Doctors are best known for their 1992 hit singles, "Little Miss Can't Be Wrong" and "Two Princes," from their 1991 album "Pocket Full of Kryptonite.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Second measles death reported as outbreak grows in Southwest
item.description: The person was unvaccinated and a resident of Lea County, New Mexico, where at least 10 cases of the disease have been reported.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Trump illegally removed labor board member
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday ruled that President Trump unlawfully removed a Democratic member of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB), suggesting in her ruling that the president sought to test the limits of his power in doing so. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: These national parks saw record attendance in 2024, but they may not tell you about it
item.description: The National Park Service has reportedly been instructed not to draw attention to the record attendance amid staffing cuts across the agency.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: A 40-day Target boycott began this week. What to know about the protest and its potential impact
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A 40-day boycott of Target that calls for supporters to give up shopping at the company's stores during the Lenten period kicked off this week, to protest the discount retailer's decision to end some of its diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why Trump's speech to Congress isn't a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: It looks like the State of the Union, and will be carried on live television, just like those annual addresses are. But it's called something else: a joint address to Congress. And it has its origins in the first term of President Ronald Reagan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
